protein_name: Tyrosine protein phosphatase which functions as a dosage-dependent inducer of mitotic progression. Directly dephosphorylates CDK1 and stimulates its kinase activity. Also dephosphorylates CDK2 in complex with cyclin E, in vitro. Belongs to the MPI phosphatase family. 2 alternatively spliced human isoforms have been reported. Note: This description may include information from UniProtKB.

raw_fields.Molecular Function: hydrolase activity; phosphoprotein phosphatase activity; protein binding; protein kinase binding; protein tyrosine phosphatase activity; protein-folding chaperone binding
raw_fields.Biological Process: cell division; cell population proliferation; G1/S transition of mitotic cell cycle; G2/M transition of mitotic cell cycle; positive regulation of cell cycle G2/M phase transition; positive regulation of DNA replication; positive regulation of G2/M transition of mitotic cell cycle; positive regulation of G2/MI transition of meiotic cell cycle; regulation of cyclin-dependent protein serine/threonine kinase activity; response to radiation
